title: LinkedIn acquisition price financing and dilution
decisionQuestion: How should Microsoft finance the $26.2 billion all-cash LinkedIn acquisition while minimizing EPS dilution?
situation: Microsoft announced the acquisition of LinkedIn at $196 per share in an all-cash deal. The transaction is expected to be slightly dilutive to non-GAAP EPS in fiscal years 2017 and 2018, becoming accretive in fiscal year 2019 or less than two years post-closing.
objectives: Achieve EPS accretion within two years
conditions: Expected close date influences dilution timeline
constraints: Acquisition must meet Microsoft's high bar for new additions

options.description: Finance primarily through new debt issued opportunistically during favorable market windows
options.expectedBenefit: Avoids equity issuance and minimizes immediate dilution; debt is accretive once LinkedIn's returns exceed interest
options.principalRisk: Rising interest rates or unfavorable market windows could increase financing costs
Immediate and permanent dilution of existing shareholders; higher bar to meet

rejectedBenefit: No interest payments from equity financing
observations: Transaction is all-cash valued at $26.2 billion inclusive of net cash
Expected dilution is about 1 percent to non-GAAP EPS in fiscal years 2017 and 2018
Accretive to non-GAAP EPS expected in fiscal year 2019 or less than two years post-close
inferences: Debt financing is the preferred lever because it defers dilution and allows the acquisition to become accretive faster than equity issuance
The 1 percent dilution is a calculated near-term cost accepted to achieve long-term accretion
Opportunistic debt issuance suggests management will time the raise to minimize interest expense

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Microsoft will acquire LinkedIn for $196 per share in an all-cash transaction valued at $26.2 billion, inclusive of LinkedIn’s net cash
Microsoft will finance the transaction primarily through the issuance of new indebtedness

Microsoft expects the acquisition to have minimal dilution of ~1 percent to non-GAAP earnings per share for the remainder of fiscal year 2017 post-closing and for fiscal year 2018 based on the expected close date, and become accretive to Microsoft’s non-GAAP earnings per share in Microsoft’s fiscal year 2019 or less than two years post-closing
we’ve agreed to acquire LinkedIn for $196 per share in an all-cash transaction that is approximately $26 billion inclusive of LinkedIn’s net cash.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: We will finance this transaction primarily through new debt issued prior to close, which we intend to raise opportunistically during favorable market windows.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: Near-term in Microsoft’s Fiscal ’17 and ’18, we anticipate this transaction will be slightly dilutive, about 1 percent, to non-GAAP EPS based on the expected close date. By FY ’19, and less than two years after close, we expect the transaction to be accretive to non-GAAP EPS.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: The addition of LinkedIn to Microsoft meets this high bar.
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood
